auto_route: decision (router picks tier; bypassed to AGENT_PROVIDER for large attachments)
notes: Decision uses the gateway's NATIVE function-calling: the model either emits a tool call (provider fills arguments as a real object) or plain text. decision.py validates the result into DecisionOutput (answer XOR tool_call). Native calling avoids the empty-{}/mangled-name failures that response_format JSON produces for an open arguments dict.

ToolCall.description:
Exactly one MCP tool invocation (INTERNAL boundary — arguments is a real
dict the loop hands to Action).
ToolCall.title: ToolCall

DecisionOutput.description:
Decision returns EITHER a final answer OR exactly one tool call —
never both, never two tools, never narration.

The model may, being a weak router-tier model, occasionally populate both
fields. The `mode="after"` validator normalizes to the invariant rather
than crashing the loop: a populated tool name wins; otherwise the answer
stands; an empty output raises (caller treats it as a failed step).
DecisionOutput.title: DecisionOutput

ToolCall.description:
Exactly one MCP tool invocation (INTERNAL boundary — arguments is a real
dict the loop hands to Action).
ToolCall.title: ToolCall
